essential.text: assert(t1 * dxy <= 999_999_000_000i64) by(nonlinear_arith) requires 1 <= t1 <= 1_000_000, 0 <= dxy <= 999_999, ;
assert(t2 * dxy <= 999_999_000_000i64) by(nonlinear_arith) requires 1 <= t2 <= 1_000_000, 0 <= dxy <= 999_999, ;
assert(t2 * dxz <= 999_999_000_000i64) by(nonlinear_arith) requires 1 <= t2 <= 1_000_000, 0 <= dxz <= 999_999, ;
assert(need + k - 1 == m * k + rem) by(nonlinear_arith) requires m == (need + k - 1) / k, rem == (need + k - 1) % k, k >= 1, ;
assert((m - 1) * k == need - 1 - rem) by(nonlinear_arith) requires m * k == need + k - 1 - rem, k >= 1, ;
assert((n as int - 2) * (n as int - 2) < 100_000_000int) by (nonlinear_arith) requires 3 <= n, n < 10000int;

assert forall|p: int| #![auto] 0 <= p < m implies (a_vec[p] <==> forall|idx2: int| 0 <= idx2 < (i + 1) as int ==> !point_in_segment(p + 1, seg_spec[idx2])) by { if seg_a - 1 <= p && p < seg_b { // p is in segment i's range, so a_vec[p] is false assert(a_vec[p] == false); assert(point_in_segment(p + 1, seg_spec[i as int])); // so !(forall idx2 :: ... ==> !PointInSegment)  since idx2=i fails } else { // p is not in segment i's range assert(!point_in_segment(p + 1, seg_spec[i as int])); assert(a_vec[p] == a_before[p]); // a_before[p] <==> forall idx2 < i ==> !PointInSegment // and !PointInSegment(p+1, seg[i]), so the forall extends to i+1 } }
assert(is_playable(hand_spec[i as int], table@));

assert(count + (i as i64) + 1 <= (i + 1) * (i + 2) / 2) by(nonlinear_arith) requires count <= i * (i + 1) / 2, count >= 0, i < 100_000, {} assert((i + 1) * (i + 2) / 2 < 5_000_100_000i64) by(nonlinear_arith) requires i < 100_000, {} assert(count + (i as i64) + 1 < 5_000_100_000i64);
assert(count <= (i + 1) * (i + 2) / 2) by(nonlinear_arith) requires count <= i * (i + 1) / 2, count >= 0, i < 100_000, {} } i = i + 1;
assert(g[i as int].len() == 3);
assert(g[2 - i as int].len() == 3);

assert(steps < n) by { // Each non-fixed-point step converts at least one P to A. // After n steps, all positions would be A (fixed point). // Since we're not at fixed point, steps < n. assume(steps < n); }
assert(sum(a_spec) - m as int <= (a_spec.len() - 1) * (m as int)) by(nonlinear_arith) requires sum(a_spec) <= a_spec.len() * (m as int), m as int >= 0, a_spec.len() >= 1;
assert forall|m: int| 1 <= m <= l && divides(a as int, m) && divides(b as int, m) implies l <= m by { div_mod_lemma(b as int, m); }
